sentence_reviews.text: Permutation-invariant or geometric collections represent objects, surfaces, particles, and unordered observations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: probability sets of related random variables, manifold learning, and geometry of quadratic forms (lines 31-35, 227-235, and 628-638); AIMA contents.html: computer vision, robotics, and mathematical background (lines 49-53, 616-619, and 768-774).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support sets, geometry/manifolds, vision, and robotics context. Point clouds, meshes, permutation invariance, surfaces, and particles are not directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Sets, point clouds & meshes is to connect Geometry, topology & symmetry, Probability theory to downstream capabilities including Robotics & embodied AI, Computer vision, perception & scene understanding.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: probability chapters and manifold learning (lines 26-72 and 628-638); AIMA contents.html: computer vision, robotics, and mathematical background (lines 49-53, 616-619, and 768-775).
sentence_reviews.notes: Probability, manifolds, computer vision, and robotics are supported. Point clouds/meshes, topology/symmetry, embodied AI, and scene understanding are only partially supported.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html: Chapter 26 'Robotics' and applications of reinforcement learning to robot control (lines 49-53 and 616-619).
sentence_reviews.notes: Robotics is directly supported, but sets/point clouds/meshes as its substrate and the embodied-AI label are not directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Geometry, topology & symmetry, Probability theory.

sentence_reviews.text: Sets, point clouds & meshes rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Geometry, topology & symmetry.

sentence_reviews.notes: Geometry/manifold support is present. Topology, symmetry, point clouds, and meshes are not directly supported in the inspected source.

sentence_reviews.text: Sets, point clouds & meshes rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: random variables, sets of related random variables, distributions, Monte Carlo approximation (lines 31-72); AIMA contents.html: Appendix A.3 probability distributions (lines 768-775).
sentence_reviews.notes: Probability is relevant for unordered observations and sampling, but the inspected sources do not directly tie probability theory to point clouds and meshes.

sentence_reviews.text: Sets, point clouds & meshes consumes, emits, or organizes information using Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: linear algebra vector spaces/matrices/manipulating data matrices and manifold learning (lines 206-259 and 628-638); AIMA contents.html: Appendix A.2 vectors/matrices/linear algebra (lines 768-774).
sentence_reviews.notes: Vector/matrix representations are supported, but their use for point clouds/meshes is synthesized from the geometry/data context.

edge_reviews.notes: Geometry/manifold evidence supports the relationship, but topology/symmetry and point-cloud/mesh specifics are not directly evidenced. The core strength is plausible but under-supported.
Probability is relevant for sets of random observations and sampling, but the dependency is not directly established for point clouds/meshes. Common strength is defensible.
Vector/matrix representations and data matrices support the represents_with/common edge as a practical encoding relation.
